description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a prominent brown gable roof and a brick-textured exterior. The front facade features a set of wide steps leading up to a main entryway, which is flanked by a bay window on one side and a covered porch or balcony above. The windows are arranged in an asymmetrical pattern, with several rectangular windows on both floors and a distinctive bay window on the lower level. The house includes a visible chimney protruding from the roof and a raised foundation. The overall design is simple, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on functional, geometric forms.
description: A two-story brick house with a brown gable roof, wide front steps, and asymmetrical windows, including a bay window and a covered porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry steps, bay window, covered porch, and multiple windows.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the sloped brown roof, with minimal visible windows or architectural details.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the house, the chimney, and the continuation of the steps and porch.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the roof and a glimpse of the bay window, but few other visible features.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with bay window and rectangular windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway with wide steps, no porch columns, and a covered upper balcony
